entry.title: Re: Billing block in VD05 is not copied to sales order.
entry.content:
Hello Akhilesh,

I could see you have opened a case with support and the engineer's reply was the bellow. Therefore, I'm adding it as an answer in the Community so others can benefit from this information as well. 🙂

"Referred to your description, it seems to be the same behavior as explained in the note 79618 - Billing block in customer master and sales order, there are two different types of billing block:

1. The billing block in the customer master is intended to generally block a customer for the billing transaction.
2. On the other hand, the billing block in the sales order is used to block only the current document for billing.

They are two kinds of field even they all call "Billing block", so this field should not be copied from customer master data into the sales order. If you would need to fulfill that requirement but do not want to set it on document type level, you may consider to apply custom logic in userexit, such as "USEREXIT_MOVE_FIELD_TO_VBAK".

For further details, you should contact your local development team or local consultants."

Kind regards,

Vic

entry.title: VAR case forwarding via SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API
entry.content:
Hello,

We saw in the new PCOE agreement that for the case forwarding, we can use an alternative solution than SAP Solution Manager (SolMan).

I saw that there is an SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API now, but it seems that we have to connect our SAP Cloud ALM tenant to SAP. My question is: how is it done? I don't see an example of Case forwarding from another ITSM to the SAP Backbone.

Do you have any information that you can share with us? We'd like to anticipate the end of SolMan and move forward with a alternative way of case forwarding. And can we use it as we are a VAR partner for all our customer?

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: VAR case forwarding via SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API is documented in:

  • API Guide for SAP Cloud ALM > ITSM API
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub > SAP Cloud ALM REST ITSM API 

Any software that can execute REST API requests can use the ITSM API. E.G. a REST API client (Postman, Bruno, etc.) can be used to manually execute requests for testing purposes.

Your external ticketing software may be able to execute REST API requests. Refer to the external ticketing software documentation to check if it offers this functionality, and how to configure it.

On the SAP side, the configuration steps for "normal" (non-VAR) Customers and VAR Customers are the same. The values provided in the JSON Request Body are the only difference. Let's examine an example of the request POST /supportcases/cases.

The Request has several mandatory Body properties. These include:

  • customerNumber
  • installationNumber
  • customer
  • reporter

A "normal" (non-VAR) Customer uses only their own data in these properties. (E.G. both "customer" and "reporter" are an S-user ID from their own company authorized to create a Case, etc.)

A VAR Customer uses their End Customer's data in most of these properties. (E.G. "customer" is an S-user ID from the End Customer, "installationNumber" is the End Customer's installation number, etc.) The "reporter" property must be an S-user ID from the VAR itself. The resulting Case will be marked as a VAR Case in the Support Backbone.

entry.title: Re: S-user Creation in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hello,

Regarding the request of creating new S-users for the company, it is necessary for a current Super Administrator to create the S-User IDs on "User Management", as explained on KBA 1271482 - How does an administrator create or delete S-user IDs? - SAP for Me.

Once the new S-users are created, the definition of new Super Administrators can be done, by a Super Admin, who must assign their own authorizations to the new Super Admin, as per on KBA 1511008 - How to add or change S-user ID authorizations - SAP for Me. 
To make it easier, the steps on KBA 1614304 - How to use the Copy Authorization feature for S-user IDs - SAP for Me can be followed, providing all information to copy all authorizations at once and providing a package to promote the users to super admins.

Thank you,
SAP Support

entry.title: Simplify how users find and share data: new AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2502)
entry.content:
The 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is here and I am excited to share with you how we are helping you achieve more, effortlessly, with new AI capabilities for business users.

These innovations are based on many interactions and feedback sessions we had with customers in 2024, including the SAP Fiori Innovation Day events in Dublin, Chicago, and Heidelberg; as well as the collaboration we had with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers during the cloud feature beta.  

Filter business data using natural language 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter enables you to set filters in SAP Fiori elements list report applications using natural language. Rather than setting each selection field manually to drill down for the business data you need, you can write a one-line query summarizing the required information – just like you would ask one of your colleagues. A large language model then interprets this query and translates it into filter values. This simplifies your information search and helps you get to the information you need faster, which can make you more productive and help you focus on the business decisions that matter. For example, imagine a busy sales manager filtering a list of top-performing accounts using a single sentence rather than walking through a variety of filters. 

This can also shorten the training time required to use different applications, as users simply search for what they need rather than needing to memorize specific selection field options.  

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter allows you to filter business data using natural language

Of course, as the user, you are free to choose how you would like to filter your list report page. Should you wish to switch between natural language filtering and the classic method, that option is available to you. Transparency is also important to us: When you use easy filter, the filter conditions (the values automatically selected for you) are displayed as filter tokens below your query. Based on your results, you can easily try a new query or manually change the filter tokens if you need to make small changes.  

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter can simplify and streamline how you find business data in your system. Just search for what you need and it will be served up to you. It’s as easy as that!  

Summarize object pages with the help of AI 

Object pages, containing the details of items from list report applications, can be complex. To help you find the information you need easily, you can use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted smart summarization to generate customizable summaries of SAP Fiori elements-based object pages. Select the sections you want to see and let smart summarization show you what you want to know. This simplifies your search for information and allows you to focus on the details that matter for a given business decision without having to scroll around to find everything you need. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted smart summarization allows you to generate summaries of SAP Fiori elements object pages

Once you have your summary generated, you can also make edits and share the summary with your colleagues. This simplifies how you share the information you need to move business processes forward.  

Personalize your SAP S/4HANA My Home page with Joule 

You can now use Joule to create cards for the Insights section of your My Home page that will give you personalized, business critical data at a glance. Rather than opening the required app, setting the required filters, and then creating the card natively in your SAP Fiori app; simply tell Joule what you need. It will create a card for you to preview and then you can add it to your My Home page with a single click to get access to essential information right when you log into the system, streamlining your decision making for the day. 

Insights card creation with Joule allows you to query for cards using natural language

Once your card is created, you can open the application directly from the card for further details, if required.  

Please note that this functionality is currently available for the following SAP Fiori applications for the 2502 release: Supplier Invoices List (Version 2), Manage Purchase Orders (Version 2), Manage Sales Orders, Manage Sales Orders - Version 2, Maintain Bill of Material, Find Maintenance Order, Find Maintenance Order and Operation, Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional, and Project Control - Enterprise Projects. 

“How can I try this out?”  

We’re very excited to get these features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition out to you! Try them out, see how they can help you find, filter, and share information. Jump into one of your SAP-delivered list report applications to try out AI-assisted easy filter. 

Click the SAP Business AI button above the filter bar to use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter

While you’re there, drill down into a business object and click the Summarization button at the top of the screen to create a quick summary of the data with AI-assisted smart summarization.  

Click the Summarize button at the top of your object page to use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted smart summarization

Strike up a conversation with Joule on your My Home page and ask to create a card with data that’s relevant to you.  

Open Joule and ask for your desired business data to be displayed as a card. This can be added to your My Home page with a single click.

Once you’ve tried filtering using natural language, summarizing your object pages, or creating your own cards – we'd love to hear what you think! Feel free to share your feedback in the comments below.  

While we wait for your feedback, we’re also working on additional AI-assisted topics to simplify how you handle error messages in your system, as well as help you create cards for your Insights section directly in My Home. We look forward to sharing them with you. 

Please note that this reflects our current state of planning and SAP reserves the right to change its development priorities at any time.  

A note on data protection in AI solutions from SAP 

We are frequently asked about what happens to your business data once it runs through an AI solution from SAP. Business data and personal information processed by SAP’s AI solutions will not be used​ by third-party LLM providers for training purposes​. If you would like to learn more about our data protection practices with AI solutions, you can refer to SAP's statement on responsible AI:​ https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-ethics.html​

Hello Akshay,

I think there is some confusion here. Ariba Parent/Child setup is for Ariba buying and invoicing (former P2P). Ariba contracts is separate module which comes with Ariba strategic sourcing suite. Ariba strategic sourcing suite supports multi-ERP setup.

Anyway, accessing contracts data via Ariba Open APIs has nothing to do with your existing CIG setup. you can access data using Open APIs irrespective of how Ariba sourcing and contracts is integrated to ERPs.

entry.title: Building Robust Enterprise Architecture: Unlocking Value Streams (In Business Context)
entry.content:
As I strengthen my expertise as a successful Enterprise Architect, I recognize that modeling Enterprise Architecture (EA) with a focus on value streams and processes is a highly effective approach for aligning business operations with customer value and strategic goals. This method enables me to identify opportunities for optimization, enhance operational efficiency, and improve organizational agility.

Understand the Core Concept of Value Streams and Processes

Before diving deep, let’s first define what we mean by value streams and processes:

Value Streams: A value stream is the series of activities an organization undertakes to deliver a product or service to customers, aiming to maximize value and minimize waste. It encompasses the flow of materials, information, and resources from concept to delivery.

Processes: Processes are a sequence of interrelated tasks performed consistently to achieve a specific outcome. Supported by systems, tools, and people, they contribute to the creation of value within a value stream.

Why Value Streams and Processes Matter in EA

Value streams and processes in EA align business goals with daily operations, improve visibility into workflows, and highlight inefficiencies. This approach promotes collaboration, reduces waste, and streamlines processes to cut costs. Real-time insights also enable faster decision-making and adaptability.

Here is a comprehensive, step-by-step approach to modeling EA through the lens of value streams and processes.

1. Define the Business Goals and Objectives

The foundation of any business architecture model is established through the clear articulation of the organization’s objectives and goals. Understand what the company aims to achieve—whether it is improving customer satisfaction, increasing operational efficiency, or driving innovation. These goals will serve as the foundation for identifying and aligning value streams and processes.

For example, a retail company may have the objective of improving customer satisfaction by making order fulfillment faster and more accurate, which in turn leads to increased loyalty and a stronger market position

2. Identify Core Value Streams

To effectively analyze and improve your organization's value stream, begin by identifying the starting and ending points to understand how value flows through the organization. Then, map the key activities that contribute to delivering the product or service. Finally, maintain a customer focus by ensuring the value stream aligns with customer needs, preferences, and desired outcomes.

Let’s add this to our retail company example: Customer Order Fulfillment is one of the core value streams for improving customer satisfaction, ensuring timely, accurate order processing and delivery while minimizing delays and errors to boost customer loyalty.

3. Identify and Map Key Processes Within Value Streams

After identifying value streams, map the processes within them, such as sales, production, and customer service. Analyze the flow to spot redundancies, inefficiencies, or bottlenecks. Signavio (Business Transformation Suite) can help by providing a platform to visualize, analyze, and optimize these processes, driving greater efficiency and transformation.

Let’s add this to our example: The key process steps inside the Customer Order Fulfillment value stream are as follows:

  • Order entry and verification (Order Received)
  • Inventory search and stock confirmation (Inventory Check)
  • Packaging and labeling (Shipping Preparation)
  • Payment authorization and fraud check (Payment Processing)
  • Shipment, tracking, and customer notification (Order Delivery)

4. Use Frameworks to Guide Implementation

To align your Enterprise Architecture with best practices and ensure scalability, leveraging frameworks like the SAP Reference Enterprise Architecture Framework is essential.

SAP Reference Architecture Framework

 

 The SAP Reference Architecture Framework is a structured framework designed to guide organizations in the deployment of SAP solutions while ensuring alignment with best practices and strategic business goals. It provides predefined templates and structures for implementing SAP systems to support various business processes.

Here, the mapped value stream is aligned with business objectives, business capabilities, SAP solutions, and KPIs within the SAP Reference Architecture framework.

 
Value Stream: Customer Order Fulfillment

 

5. Visualize the EA

Visualization of Enterprise Architecture (EA) in tools like SAP LeanIX helps represent complex systems and align business objectives, value streams, capabilities, SAP solutions, and KPIs. LeanIX uses factsheets to model these elements, providing a structured approach for better decision-making and communication across the organization.

By defining business objectives and linking them to value stream steps, each step connects to business capabilities via Capability Factsheets. These are mapped to SAP solutions through Application Factsheets, while KPIs are tracked using Metric Factsheets, ensuring alignment and clarity in achieving business goals.

 EAM in SAP LeanIX

 6. Keep Iterating

Business architecture is an ongoing process that requires continuous refinement to adapt to the ever-changing business environment. To ensure effectiveness, organizations should use Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to measure metrics such as customer satisfaction, efficiency, and cost.

Regularly revisiting the business architecture model allows for identification of improvement opportunities, and gathering feedback from stakeholders across the organization ensures informed adjustments that drive ongoing optimization and growth.

 To sum up, modeling business architecture with value streams and processes improves efficiency, aligns operations with goals, and enhances customer value. A structured, iterative approach drives success and growth while optimizing resources.

entry.title: Re: S/4 OnPrem - Is Key User Extensibility "Clean Core" b...
entry.content:
Hi  @Marçal_Oliveras 

ABAP Cloud is the one common development model to build cloud-ready extensions and  applications, both on-stack and side-by-side scenarios. The ABAP Cloud development model builds on the following key concepts:

  • Usage of released APIs only
  • Cloud-optimized ABAP language
  • Cloud-optimized architecture

The development model covers both, key user and developer extensibility. The cloud-optimized ABAP language versions are „ABAP for Cloud Development” and “ABAP for Key Users”.

So yes, key user extensibility is part of ABAP Cloud and is clean core.

Regarding the organization of software components and packages:

  • For developer extensibility, you develop in a software component of language version ABAP for Cloud Development, for instance ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT. All objects in that software component must have the ABAP for Cloud Development language version. Objects in that software component can use any other object of that software component, and objects from other software components that are released for use in cloud development. The development objects are maintained using ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse.
  • For key user extensibility, you use a software component of language version Standard ABAP, typically HOME. The extensions are maintained via Fiori apps. Based on the user input, the apps will generate various development objects that make up the extension. As you already mentioned, the objects are created in a local package and then need to be moved to a package of that software component. The objects have either language version Standard ABAP or ABAP for Key Users. However, you do not need to worry: the apps will take care that everything is clean core, for instance, by restricting access: a key user extension can only access objects that are released for key user apps, or objects that were created by key user apps and were marked for use by the apps.
  • For classic extensibility, you develop in a software component of language version Standard ABAP, typically HOME. You create development objects in the Standard ABAP language version with the tool of your choice, for instance the SE80 transaction.

In a nutshell: for developer extensibility you need your own package(s) in a cloud development software component. You can use the HOME software component for key user and classic extensibility. However, I highly recommend using different packages for key user and classic extensibility.

Best regards
Kai

entry.title: EWM RF – Implement custom code in standard RF transaction

entry.title: Re: Importing Entities with Semantic of CDS views from S/4HANA On-Premise in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @MKreitlein ,

Thank you for your comment! I completely agree with you and I also couldn't find anything when I started exploring this so I published this blog. My opinion is that it is not used as it doesn't bring all the associations due to the complexity of the problem such as CDS views has to be extraction enabled to appear in the Datasphere so if the top level CDS view is extraction enabled but the underlying associations are not, then it will become local table. Secondly, associations could be multi-folded so it would create objects which are not fully exposing all the associations from the back-end CDS views. Due to these reasons, I guess its easier to create from scratch with the requirements 

Please share your thoughts

Regards,

Armaan

entry.title: Need to assign app variant to users
entry.content:
Hi,

We have created a variant for the app "My Purchase Orders - Due for Delivery" app. We want to check if it is possible to assign this app variant to specific users. 

 

Thank you!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need to assign app variant to users
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

When you create an App Variant it will be assigned to a Custom Catalog Extension on which you can choose the Business Catalog that will provide access to this variant. Once you choose the catalog, the users with the roles containing the chosen catalog will have access to the variant.

The step-by-step is:

  1. In the "Adapt UI" screen, choose "Manage App Variants" and copy the ID of the app variant to the clipboard.
  2. Open the Custom Catalog Extensions app. Search for the app variant using the copied ID from your clipboard.
  3. By default, the Business Catalog will be the same as the catalog that provides authorization to the standard app. If you want to change it, you need to select the Business Catalog and click on "Revoke" and after that, remove the catalog. Then, you click on "Add" to add a new catalog.
  4. Publish the new catalog

 

For more information and step-by-step, please check the following documentations:

SAP Help Portal | App Variants: All you Need to Know
SAP Help Portal | Working with App Variants
SAP Help Portal | Publishing App Variants

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: Adding MRP controller number to Purchase order number
entry.content: Does acknowledgement update on PO line item ? if yes --you can easily create report that MRP Controllers rely on or--use RPA or BOT--that notify the MRP Controllers ---(They no need to look group email inbox to search for the PO 😉 ) MRP Controller is on material level and which makes is PO line dependent-- no header level - i understand you want to quickly identify the POs that acknowledgement -- why not you create some sort BOT or RPA-- which read the PO number received from the group email --check the PO line items and material codes on them and relevant MRP Controllers -- and create Email for those MRP Controllers-- This can easily achieved using RPA or BOT -instead influencing the PO number range and making it too much complicated for simple requirement.

entry.title: MD04 collective access - adding custom text field
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Is there a way to add custom field into collective access table? In this field I would like to have the ability to type (10 or less symbols would be enought) and this short note would be then saved to database so it is not lost when I close the MD04 transaction or leave SAP.

This would be great addition for MRP controllers, since they could for example mark last time they checked certain material or add small hints. There is the AC(already accessed) field that checkmarks the material you already openned, but this is lost once you close MD04 and not beneficial enough.

Example of how it could look. From collective access, you select parts you are interested in, then choose Selected stock and show Overview tree, while in Overview tree, you inherit the fields from the first table in collective access and there you would add priority 3 to this custom text field, so it is visible while checking materials.

entry.title: Mapping Rules in Cloud Foundary
entry.content:
No regular expression in mapping rules in SAP BTP CF, it was found as attached in SAP BTP NEO but we cannot find this option in CF 

Where can we find it for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry?

We need to map groups from the identity provider into roles from SAP BTP.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mapping Rules in Cloud Foundary
entry.content:
Hello!

In order to map groups or attributes into Roles, you need to refer to Mapping Role Collections in the Subaccount.

In summary:

  • You can either directly assign each user to the required Role Collections - Assign Users to Role Collections
  • Or you can setup a logic to map the Role Collections to specific Groups or Attributes from the Custom Identity Provider - Map Role Collections to User Attributes and Map Role Collections to User Groups.

 

Best regards,
Vini
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: RAP - ListReport - Create button not appearing on Lin...
entry.content:
Hi @chengalarayulu 

In your ROOT Behavior Definition's Consumption View you may need to add association like below.

 

use association <Child Entity Association Name> { create; with draft; }

 

 In ROOT Entity Interface View change Association like below.

 

association <Child Entity Association Name> { create; with draft; }

 

Do not add 'with draft;' If you have not Implemented Draft capability.

 

Hope this helps-

Thanks-

Then double click the one and only AND to change it to an OR. Your original screen capture has 3 extra ANDs between each condition with nothing associated with them. It should look like this:
